grantTitle: EIT Higher Education Initiative — Call for Proposals 2025
grantingOrganization: European Institute of Innovation and Technology (EIT)
summary: The EIT HEI 2025 Call aims to boost STEM education and strengthen innovation capacity across Europe, targeting 200,000 students, academics and staff by 2028. Projects can focus on either developing innovation/entrepreneurship capacity in STEM education (Topic 1) or strengthening synergies between European university alliances and EIT Knowledge and Innovation Communities (Topic 2). Total funding of EUR 70 million is available for 35-40 projects over 24 months.
objectives: Reinforce Europe's innovation and entrepreneurship capacity in STEM education
Support the EU's Union of Skills and STEM Education Strategic Plan
Train 200,000 students, academics and non-academic staff in innovation, entrepreneurship and intellectual property management by 2028
Integrate entrepreneurship, IP management, and research commercialization into STEM curricula
Develop innovation and entrepreneurial capacity in STEM talent pipeline
Build strategic synergies between European university alliances and EIT Knowledge and Innovation Communities
Unite university alliances with research and business community involvement

requirements.description: Minimum consortium of 5 partner organizations total
requirements.evidence: List of consortium partners with roles

requirements.description: At least 3 higher education institutions (HEIs) from 3 different Horizon Europe-eligible countries
requirements.evidence: Institutional accreditation documents for each HEI; proof of legal registration in Horizon Europe eligible countries

requirements.description: At least 1 business partner (SME, corporate, or investor)
requirements.evidence: Business partner registration and commitment letter

requirements.description: At least 1 additional non-HEI partner (research organization, civil society, or public body)
Topic 2 Special Requirement: All 3 HEIs must be members of the same European Universities alliance established under the European Universities Initiative, or were members after 1 January 2019
requirements.evidence: Proof of membership in European Universities alliance

requirements.description: All partners must be legally registered in Horizon Europe eligible countries
Innovation Vision Action Plan (IVAP) using provided Part B template
requirements.evidence: Completed Part B template (separate for Topic 1 and Topic 2)

requirements.description: HEInnovate self-assessment completion by each participating university
requirements.evidence: HEInnovate self-assessment results from each HEI

requirements.description: Top management commitment from HEI leadership
requirements.evidence: Signed commitment letters from university leadership listing participating faculties/departments
Signed commitment letters from business partners

requirements.description: National accreditation proof for each HEI
Participant Identification Codes (PIC) for all organizations
requirements.evidence: PIC registration in EU Participant Portal

requirements.description: Financial plan and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)
requirements.evidence: Completed budget template (Excel format) with KPIs

requirements.description: Explanation of complementarity with other EU funding (HORIZON-WIDERA, Erasmus+) to avoid double funding
requirements.evidence: Written explanation in application; duplication risks automatic exclusion

evaluationCriteria.name: Admissibility Check
evaluationCriteria.description: First stage evaluation to ensure application meets basic submission requirements

evaluationCriteria.name: Eligibility Check
evaluationCriteria.description: Second stage evaluation to verify consortium composition and partner eligibility
keyIndicators: Minimum consortium requirements met (5 partners total)
All partners legally registered in Horizon Europe eligible countries
Topic 2: HEIs are members of same European Universities alliance

evaluationCriteria.name: External Evaluation
evaluationCriteria.description: Third stage comprehensive evaluation of project quality and impact (conducted only if admissibility and eligibility checks pass)
keyIndicators: Sustainability and scalability of proposed activities

categories.description: Maximum funding per individual project over 24 months

categories.category: Innovation Action Plan Development and Implementation
categories.description: Funding for developing and implementing innovation action plans
Funding for designing and delivering entrepreneurship programs
Funding for training and mentoring students, academics, and staff
Funding for establishing new innovation structures within institutions
Funding for building partnerships and innovation ecosystems
Funding for technology transfer activities and supporting startups

deliverables: All partners registered with PIC codes
Selected projects notified with evaluation reports and launch instructions
Training and mentoring of students, academics and staff

targetBeneficiaries: Non-academic staff in higher education institutions
Startups and entrepreneurs in STEM fields
geographicScope: European Union and Horizon Europe eligible countries
eligibleCountries: All Horizon Europe eligible countries (EU Member States and Associated Countries)
additionalNotes: The initiative aims to train 200,000 students, academics and non-academic staff by 2028
Two distinct topics available: Topic 1 focuses on boosting innovation/entrepreneurship capacity; Topic 2 focuses on synergies between university alliances and EIT KICs
Previous participants from Cohorts 1-4 are encouraged to apply as 'Innovation & Entrepreneurship Pioneers' to mentor new partners
Applicants must demonstrate complementarity with other EU funding programs (HORIZON-WIDERA, Erasmus+) to avoid double funding risks
Each HEI must complete a HEInnovate self-assessment as part of the application
Top management commitment is required with signed letters listing participating faculties/departments
Application requires 11 steps including consortium formation, document preparation, and portal submission
Extensive support resources available: 7 recorded info sessions, FAQs, interactive eligibility guide, KIC synergy documents
EIT Manufacturing and other EIT KICs are involved in administering the call
Selected projects will join the EIT HEI community post-selection
National accreditation is mandatory for each participating HEI
Application deadline is strict: 4 March 2026, 17:00 CET
